rejected_endpoints.reason: All 3 match IDs returned 404; endpoint may not exist or may need /data/ prefix
ccode3 parameter did not change 404 result
timezone parameter did not change 404 result

historical_files_reviewed.description: Phase 5.21 L2V3S detail API endpoint feasibility analysis
Phase 5.12 L2B detail route selector (favors html_hydration over api_match_details)
Phase 5.12 L2A raw detail access route read-only audit
ADG2 anti-bot differential diagnosis (page vs API discrepancy)
Live fetch route review script with endpoint construction logic

endpoint_candidates.evidence_summary: 9 requests across 3 match IDs all returned 404
endpoint_candidates.reason: Rejected: 9/9 404 responses in #1439. Endpoint missing /data/ segment.

endpoint_candidates.evidence_summary: Production FotMobApiClient.fetchMatchDetails() uses /api/data/matchDetails. Phase 5.21L2V3S observed HTTP 403 (blocked by Cloudflare). MarathonService._buildMatchDetailsApiUrls() also references this endpoint. This is the canonical FotMob detail JSON API but requires session/cookies.
endpoint_candidates.reason: Canonical endpoint from production code. May return 403 without session but is the correct URL pattern. Worth probing to confirm status code.

endpoint_candidates.evidence_summary: titan_seeder.js uses ccode3=USA with /api/data/leagues endpoint. Adding ccode3 to the /api/data/ prefix may help with geo-based content negotiation.
endpoint_candidates.reason: /api/data/ prefix variant with geo code. Not yet tested. Lower confidence.

endpoint_candidates.evidence_summary: FotMobRawDetailFetcher.buildFotMobMatchUrl() constructs /match/{externalId}. Returns HTTP 200 with __NEXT_DATA__ embedded in page HTML. This is the HTML hydration route used by the production pageProps v2 pipeline. Phase ADG60 confirmed 32/32 targets accessible via this route. Requires parsing <script id='__NEXT_DATA__'> from HTML response. Note: this returns HTML, not JSON; metadata probe would detect content-type=text/html.
endpoint_candidates.reason: Highest confidence endpoint. Known HTTP 200. Requires pageProps extraction but URL pattern is validated.

endpoint_candidates.evidence_summary: ADG60 live fetch used /matches/{hash}/{externalId} format. Returns HTTP 200 with SSR pageProps. This is the same URL pattern as user-supplied known match pages. Hash/route_code from seed parsing can be used directly.
endpoint_candidates.reason: ADG60-validated route. Uses route_code from user seeds. Returns HTML with embedded pageProps.

endpoint_candidates.evidence_summary: User-supplied known match page URLs follow this pattern. Returns HTTP 200, SSR pageProps embedded. This is the exact URL users provided. Note: returns HTML page, not JSON API.
endpoint_candidates.reason: User seeds use this exact pattern. Known to return 200. HTML, not JSON.

endpoint_candidates.evidence_summary: FotMobStrategy._fetchMatchDetailsWithSession() uses /api/data/matchDetails with Playwright browser session cookies. This is the production L2 harvesting path. Requires browser automation (Playwright), session cookies, and proxy rotation. DEFERRED: cannot be probed in current no-write, no-browser phase.
endpoint_candidates.reason: Deferred: requires browser automation, session cookies, and proxy. Not for current no-write phase.

endpoint_candidates.evidence_summary: Historical GSM compressed JSON endpoint referenced in backfill scripts. May be deprecated or require specific match ID format. DEFERRED: endpoint format unclear, may not return current data.
endpoint_candidates.reason: Deferred: historical endpoint, may be deprecated. Review before probing.

stop_conditions: 403 on any endpoint → stop that endpoint immediately
429 on any endpoint → stop all probes immediately
content_type=text/html → record as html_hydration, do NOT parse body
content_type=application/json → record top-level keys only, do NOT save body
next_probe_plan.next_phase_notes: Next phase should probe /api/data/matchDetails (the canonical endpoint from production code), /match/{match_id} (HTML hydration route, known 200), and /matches/{route_code}/{match_id} (SSR page, known 200). All three use the /data/ prefix or page routes that differ from the #1439 404 endpoints. If /api/data/matchDetails returns 403, that confirms anti-bot blocking; the HTML hydration routes should return 200 but with text/html content_type.
